title: Sci-Fi Anomalies & Phenomena
description: Strange occurrences, unexplained readings, and unsettling discoveries in deep space and on frontier worlds — the texture of a universe that doesn't always behave.

entries.result: A navigation beacon is transmitting on a frequency that hasn't been assigned to any vessel, station, or installation in the sector database.
A region of space where all scans return null data — not interference, not malfunction. Simply nothing, in a volume that should have something.
A ship's chronometer loses exactly seventeen minutes, station-synchronized, during a routine transit. Every clock on board, simultaneously.
A debris field arranged in a pattern that suggests a deliberate structure rather than a collision event. The pattern repeats at different scales.
A low-frequency signal emanating from a planet's surface that began three weeks ago. No structure has been identified at the origin point.
A gas giant's storm system has changed rotation direction. It has maintained the same direction of rotation for the entire period of observation, spanning centuries.
Gravity readings in a corridor aboard the station are consistently 0.3% higher than in adjacent sections, with no mechanical explanation.
A comet's trajectory does not match its observed mass and velocity. Something is affecting its path that instruments cannot identify.
A section of space where objects slow down — not dramatically, but measurably. Ships passing through lose a few seconds relative to surrounding space.
An uninhabited moon is producing oxygen in quantities inconsistent with its geological activity. The oxygen began increasing six months ago.
A star's spectral output has shifted slightly outside its expected band. The shift is small but consistent and has been increasing for two years.
Every automated probe sent into a specific asteroid field returns without data and with its memory wiped, but otherwise undamaged.
A ship's AI begins completing sentences that its crew hasn't finished — not through context prediction, but with information the AI has no source for.
A previously uninhabited system shows thermal signatures consistent with waste heat from industrial processes. No authorized settlement exists there.
Identical crystalline formations have been found on three planets in three separate systems, with no geological mechanism that could produce the same structure in all three.
Crew members on a long-haul vessel begin reporting the same dream on the same night. The dream contains accurate details about events none of them witnessed.
A planet's magnetic poles have shifted in a pattern that precisely corresponds to a prime number sequence.
A region of space where electrical systems experience random failures — not uniformly, but following a pattern that takes time to recognize as intentional.
A moon's surface shows signs of recent erosion, but the erosion pattern suggests liquid water on a world with no water.
A derelict vessel found in open space has all systems offline — except one interior light, in one compartment, which is on.
A binary star system where the two stars are drifting apart faster than orbital mechanics predicts, with no tidal or collision event to explain it.
A station's life support system begins producing a gas mixture that is breathable, safe, and completely absent from its configuration settings.
A signal received from outside the known boundary of explored space, formatted as a standard distress call, in a language used three hundred years ago.
A planet's day-night cycle has shortened by eleven minutes over the past two years. The rate of shortening is accelerating.
Three separate ships passing the same region report seeing an identical vessel that is not present on any registry and does not respond to hails.
A wormhole that does not appear on any chart, stable and navigable, at the edge of a system that has been catalogued for decades.
A colony's agricultural output has doubled without any change to crop, soil treatment, or climate management. The farmers have no explanation.
A system-wide scan picks up a signal buried in background radiation that, when isolated, encodes a complete star map — of a region that doesn't match any known location.
A ship's hull has developed microscopic scoring in a pattern that, at the right scale, resolves into text in no known language.
A planet previously classified as uninhabitable has developed a breathable atmosphere with no recorded geological event to account for the change.
All biological samples collected on a specific world decay at exactly half the normal rate, regardless of storage conditions.
A black hole's accretion disk has developed a persistent gap — a region where matter is not present and where matter directed into the gap does not appear.
An asteroid is warm. Not from solar radiation, not from tidal heating. Just warm, and slightly warmer on one face than the other.
A neutrino burst was detected from a system where no supernova occurred. The burst was brief, focused, and directional.
Plants brought aboard a vessel from a new world are growing toward a fixed point in space rather than toward the nearest light source.
A planet's surface has a region where compasses — magnetic, gravitational, or inertial — all point to the same fixed location rather than to their calibrated reference points.
A decommissioned comm relay has resumed broadcasting. The broadcast is on a standard frequency. The content is static interrupted by regular silence at intervals that form a pattern.
A region of space where objects lose angular momentum — spinning items slow and stop, regardless of the conservation laws that should prevent this.
A vessel that was reported destroyed eight years ago appears on long-range scan, in good condition, moving under its own power.
A world's indigenous animal population has stopped reproducing. Not declining — stopped entirely, simultaneously, with no disease or environmental cause identified.
Water in a settlement on a new colony has begun crystallizing at temperatures well above its freezing point. The crystallization produces consistent hexagonal structures that are not ice.
A system's gas giant is visible from the outer planets with the naked eye at a brightness it should not be capable of producing.
A section of a ship's hull has become transparent — not damaged, not replaced — the original material, which is not transparent, has become so.
A colony's records show that their last three years of births all occurred on the same calendar date. Not clustered — every single birth.
An electromagnetic pulse is emitted from the core of a station at precisely thirty-six-hour intervals, disrupting electronics within a three-meter radius of the center of the station.
A nebula that was mapped twenty years ago no longer occupies the position it was charted at. The nebula is present, but moved.
A planet's tectonic activity has completely ceased. No quakes, no volcanic activity, no geological movement of any kind. For nine years.
A ship in dock has developed a resonant frequency that perfectly matches a specific transmission received from deep space eleven months ago.
Animals in a region of a new world are traveling in a single direction, apparently migrating, toward a point in the ocean where there is no land or food source.
A deep space telescope captures an image of a structure orbiting a distant star. The structure is geometric. The star is four hundred light-years away.
A region of a world where electronic memory — ship computers, personal devices, recording equipment — stores nothing. The devices work, but retain no data from time spent in the region.
A habitable world's ocean has changed color in a region three kilometers across. The change happened overnight. The water is chemically unchanged.
A star's light in one narrow band of the spectrum has been blocked at precise intervals over the past six months. The pattern is non-random.
A ship's sensor array detects a mass reading in open space — consistent with a vessel of significant size — where nothing is visually present.
A planet that should be geologically dead is showing new volcanic activity — but only in perfectly straight lines, precisely one hundred kilometers apart.
Time aboard a specific station advances at a measurably slower rate than aboard ships in the same orbit. The rate of drift is increasing.
A world's northern hemisphere experiences simultaneous aurora activity — not the standard polar aurora, but a full-hemisphere display that lasts forty-eight hours and recurs every seventeen days.
A vessel emerges from jump several hours early. The crew experienced the full expected duration. The discrepancy is unexplained.
A moon has developed a ring system that wasn't present eighteen months ago. The ring material doesn't match any component of the moon's known geology.
Animals near a crater on a new world have developed identical markings — not species-wide, only in individuals within two kilometers of the crater.
A region of space where drive systems underperform by exactly forty percent. Not complete failure — consistent, measurable, forty percent.
A gas that cannot be identified by standard spectrographic analysis is present in the atmosphere of an otherwise well-characterized world.
A star has begun flickering in a pattern that, if interpreted as binary, translates to the coordinates of a location within the current system.
Every plant species in a specific valley on a colony world has oriented its growth to face a single point underground, as if growing toward a light source that doesn't exist.
A previously stable orbit has become unstable. A moon is slowly spiraling inward. The timeline is centuries, but the cause is unknown.
A communication intercept catches a fragment of an old mission's final transmission — from a mission that was lost forty years ago — broadcast on a current, active frequency.
A region of space where FTL calculations refuse to resolve. Every attempt to plot a jump through the region produces an error the navigation systems cannot specify.
A planet's rotation has changed. Slowly, over decades, but measurably. The day is now seventeen minutes shorter than records from fifty years ago indicate.
A hollow has been found in a planet's ice shelf — perfectly spherical, large enough for a ship, with no entry point.
A species on a newly catalogued world has no DNA. Its biology is functional and complex, but there is no genetic material in any cell.
A region on a station where sound doesn't carry — not suppressed, not muffled. Words spoken aloud simply don't propagate more than two meters.
A vessel exits jump at the wrong coordinates — not by much, but enough. The jump was correctly calculated. The discrepancy can't be explained by instrument error.
A planet's shadow is slightly wrong — the terminator line doesn't match the sun's position by a consistent fraction of a degree.
A section of a station that was decommissioned and sealed has begun registering atmosphere, temperature, and motion on passive sensors.
A signal detected from a probe sent twenty years ago — the probe was destroyed in transit, officially. The signal is current.
A ship leaving a system accelerates beyond its drive specifications for thirty seconds, then returns to normal performance. The drive logs show nominal output throughout.
A world's largest continent has a region where nothing decays. Organic matter placed there does not decompose. Samples brought from the region resume normal decay immediately.
A previously uncharted structure appears in a system's asteroid belt — artificial geometry, no thermal signature, no electronic emissions.
A star is emitting light in the visible spectrum, as expected, but also emitting an identical pattern of radiation in a band that no known stellar process produces.
A species on a colony world has begun exhibiting behavior it did not show during initial survey — specifically, avoiding a location it previously nested in, for no apparent reason.
A ship's reactor output decreases during transit through a specific corridor of space. Returns to normal on exit. No cause has been identified in six separate transits.

A transmission received from across the galaxy — functionally impossible at current technology — in a format consistent with a standard message relay, sent by a source that doesn't exist in the registry.
A planet's polar ice caps are sublimating, but only on one hemisphere, and only at night.
A derelict station found in deep space has a single compartment that is warm — not powered warm, metabolically warm.
A moon orbiting a gas giant has begun emitting a structured radio signal. The structure is too complex to be geological and too simple to be linguistic.
A region of a new world where insects don't enter and animals avoid. No chemical, biological, or physical deterrent has been identified.
Crew members aboard a vessel on extended transit begin reporting memories of events that did not occur during the voyage — specific, shared memories.
An electromagnetic anomaly causes all chronometers in a region to agree with each other but disagree with synchronized time from the nearest relay. By the same amount.
A comet that should have impacted a planet thirty years ago did not. It passed through the predicted impact zone and continued as if the planet were not there. It is confirmed on record.
A settlement's power grid draws exactly 2% less energy than its metered consumption every day, with no unexplained draws and no identified efficiency.
An abandoned research outpost's logs show entries from three weeks after the date the crew was evacuated.
A gas giant's internal pressure produces compounds at its core that, by current understanding of chemistry, cannot form at those pressures.
A navigational buoy has been transmitting accurate, real-time position data for a region of space it is not located in.
A planet's ocean floor has a structure at depth that sonar describes as a regular grid. The depth makes direct survey impossible with current equipment.
A region of space where ships' power systems read full charge regardless of actual state — the power is there, but the instruments believe it has never been used.
An uninhabited world shows evidence of atmospheric processing — the composition is too hospitable to be natural, and there is no known installation there.
A species encountered on a new world is biologically identical to a species from a world on the other side of the known galaxy. Down to the genome.

A transit corridor widely used for generations produces, in one specific location, an identical hallucination in every person who passes through it — described consistently across hundreds of separate reports.
An asteroid has been following a ship. Not intercepting — following, at a consistent distance, matching course corrections with a lag that suggests reaction rather than coincidence.

All data collected on a world over the past decade — sensors, logs, samples, recordings — tells a consistent story of a normal, unremarkable planet. The planet does not match that description in any detail.
